title: Fluffy Indulgent Argentinian Dried Fruit Empanadas
description: Earthly argentinian empanadas filled with dried fruit and nuka pickle, made with shojin friendly ingredients, creating an indulgent treat.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, sugar, and salt.
Add the melted butter, egg replacer mixture, and vanilla extract to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of dried fruit mix and a teaspoon of nuka pickle in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half over the filling and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little bit of water and bake for 20 minutes or until golden brown.
Refrigerate and consume within 1 week — this preparation is not shelf-stable without an approved acid level and canning process.

Refrigerator pickle only: store in the fridge and consume within 1 week. For shelf-stable canning, add at least 2 tablespoons of vinegar (5% acidity) per cup of brine and follow a tested water-bath process.
